aging: verb. present participle and gerund of age
ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
and: conjunction. As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
dag: noun. A hanging end or shred, in particular a long pointed strip of cloth at the edge of a piece of clothing, or one of a row of decorative
daring: verb. present participle and gerund of dare
dig: verb. To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dinar: noun. The official currency of several countries, including Algeria, Bahrain, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Libya, Serbia, Tunisia and (as denar)
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
drag: noun. Resistance of a fluid to something moving through it.
drain: noun. A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole
gad: interjection. An exclamation roughly equivalent to by God, goodness gracious, for goodness' sake.
gadi: noun. A cushioned throne used by Indian princes.
gag: noun. A device to restrain speech, such as a rag in the mouth secured with tape or a rubber ball threaded onto a cord or strap.
gaging: verb. present participle and gerund of gage
gar: noun. Any of several North American fish of the family Lepisosteidae that have long, narrow jaws.
gid: noun. A disease caused by coenurosis of the brain, most commonly found in sheep and canids.
gig: noun. Originally, a performing engagement by a musician or musical group; a job or role for a performer.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
gird: verb. To bind with a flexible rope or cord.
gradin: noun. Any of a series of terraced steps or seats, as in an arena or an altar.
grading: verb. present participle and gerund of grade
grain: noun. The harvested seeds of various grass food crops eg: wheat, corn, barley.
grand: adjective. Large, senior (high-ranking), intense, extreme, or exceptional Of a large size or extent; great.
grid: noun. A rectangular array of squares or rectangles of equal size, such as in a crossword puzzle.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
grind: verb. To reduce to smaller pieces by crushing with lateral motion.
nadir: noun. The point of the celestial sphere, directly opposite the zenith; inferior pole of the horizon; point of the celestial sphere directly
nag: noun. A small horse; a pony.
nard: noun. A flowering plant of species Nardostachys jatamansi, in the valerian family, that grows in the Himalayas and is used as a perfume, an
ragging: verb. present participle and gerund of rag
ragi: noun. A grain, of species Eleusine coracana, cultivated as a cereal in arid areas of Africa and Asia; finger millet.
raging: verb. present participle and gerund of rage
raid: noun. A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.
rain: noun. Condensed water falling from a cloud.
rand: noun. The border of an area of land, now especially marshland.
rani: noun. The wife of a rajah.
ranid: adjective. Of or pertaining to the true frogs.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
agrin: adjective. Grinning; having happiness or satisfaction apparent on one's face.
ard: noun. A simple plough consisting of a spike dragged through the soil.
arg: noun. An argument; a value passed as a parameter.
arni: noun. The wild water buffalo (Bubalus arnee)
dagging: verb. present participle and gerund of dag
dan: noun. A title of honour or respect similar to "master" or "Sir", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
dar: noun. A fish found in the Severn River; a dart or dace.
gari: noun. Alternative form of garri (“West African food made from cassava”).
ggr: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Aghu Tharnggalu.
ging: noun. A company; troop; a gang.
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
inga: noun. Any of the genus Inga of small tropical trees and shrubs.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
rna: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Runa.
